prerequisites: A Firebolt account on AWS (Firebolt runs on AWS infrastructure).
A database created in your Firebolt account, populated with data you want to sync.
A Firebolt engine that is running. Stopped or booting engines cannot accept connections.

A Firebolt service account, with its client ID and client secret, associated with a user that has role privileges to access the target database and engine. You provide the engine name (engine) and database name (database) in the connector config; host is optional for a custom endpoint.

authMethods.name: Service account (OAuth 2.0 client credentials)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a Firebolt service account using the client_id and client_secret config fields (client_secret is the only secret). Firebolt exchanges these credentials for a temporary bearer access token via the OAuth 2.0 client_credentials grant at https://id.app.firebolt.io/oauth/token. The token response returns expires_in of 86400 seconds (24 hours). The service account must be linked to a user holding the role privileges needed to reach the configured engine and database.

setupSteps.title: Create a Firebolt account, database, and engine
setupSteps.body: Sign up for Firebolt on AWS, create a database, and load your data into it. Create or start an engine and confirm it is in a running state, since the connector cannot connect through a stopped or booting engine. Note the engine name and database name for the engine and database config fields.

setupSteps.title: Create a service account and generate credentials
setupSteps.body: In the Firebolt UI, go to Configure Space > Service accounts > Create service account, or run CREATE SERVICE ACCOUNT in SQL. Generate its secret using the Rotate secret action (copy the secret immediately, as it cannot be retrieved later) or by calling fb_GENERATESERVICEACCOUNTKEY in SQL. The service account ID becomes client_id and the generated secret becomes client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Associate the service account with a user that has access
setupSteps.body: Create or pick a user with the role privileges required to use the target database and engine, and associate the service account with that user. Without an associated user holding the right privileges, the credentials cannot reach the configured engine or database.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter your Firebolt account identifier (account), the service account ID (client_id), and the generated secret (client_secret). Set database to the target database name and engine to the running engine name. Optionally set host to a custom endpoint; otherwise the default is used.

sources.title: Airbyte: Firebolt source connector (prerequisites and setup)
